title: NOC 51111 – Authors and Writers (except Technical) | GTR Immigration
description: Authors and Writers (except Technical) under NOC 51111 are professional creative and content specialists responsible for producing written material for publication, broadcast, advertising, and digital media across Canada. Classified under TEER 1, this occupation usually requires a university degree and strong writing ability. These professionals are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group51 – Professional occupations in art and cultureSub-Major Group511 – Professional occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5111 – Writing, translating and related communications professionalsNOC Code51111

duties: Authors and Writers (except Technical) perform some or all of the following duties:
Novelists, Playwrights, Script Writers, Poets, and Creative Writers
Conceive and write novels, plays, scripts, poetry, and other creative material
Conduct research to ensure factual accuracy
Develop storylines, characters, and thematic concepts
Write blog posts, e-books, podcasts, and web-based content
Consult with clients to determine content objectives
Write advertising copy and promotional materials
Develop persuasive messaging for commercials and campaigns
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in English, French, marketing, advertising, journalism, or a related discipline is usually required
Creative writing programs are offered by universities and colleges
A strong portfolio of published work is an important hiring criterion
Membership in a writers’ guild or union may be required in certain cases
salary_prospects: Authors and Writers (except Technical) typically earn:
$36,600 – $90,700 per year,depending on specialization, province, and employment type.
With 23,100 projected job openings compared to 19,300 job seekers, NOC 51111 reflects strong employment prospects in Canada.
Rapid expansion of online and multimedia content
Growth in marketing and branding industries
Opportunities are strongest in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, and Quebec.
NOC 51111 is classified under TEER 1, making it generally eligible under:
Eligibility depends on education credential assessment (ECA), language proficiency, and skilled work experience.

Guide you through documentation and credential evaluation

labour_market_demand: Expansion of digital media and online content
Growth in advertising and corporate communications
Replacement demand due to workforce turnover
With significantly more job openings than job seekers projected, employment prospects are considered strong.
Opportunities in digital, print, and broadcast media
Growing demand in content marketing and branding
Writers play a key role in shaping Canada’s media, advertising, publishing, and entertainment industries.
